## Definition

Membrane proteins serve as functional gateways and signal recognition units of the cell, including ion channels, transporter proteins, and receptor proteins that mediate movement of substances and signals across the cell membrane.

## Core Principles

- *Ion channels* are membrane proteins with pores through which ions pass into and out of the cell via *diffusion*.
- *Transporter proteins* actively move substances into and out of the cell via *facilitated transport*.
- *Receptor proteins* recognize extracellular signals (typically through hormone binding), which in turn alters intracellular functions.

## Sub-concepts

- Ion channels
- Transporter proteins
- Receptor proteins
